hypothesis: Incorporating DFT+U corrections specifically calibrated for 3d transition metal MOFs as an intermediate-fidelity level within a multi-fidelity ∆-learning cascade (GFN1-xTB → PBE+U → HSE06) will yield greater predictive accuracy for band gaps of transition-metal-containing MOFs than the two-level ∆-learning framework demonstrated here, because the explicit treatment of strong d-electron correlation at the intermediate level will reduce the systematic errors that currently cause GFN1-xTB to underestimate band gaps for MOFs containing Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, and Ni, thereby providing a more informative correction signal for the machine learning model to learn.
